title: Expose normalized result metadata for hive-mind solve sessions
body:
During hive-mind PR https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1044 for issue https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1043, the experimental `--use-agent-commander` adapter had to best-effort parse raw/plain output from `agent-commander@0.4.3` to populate hive-mind's solve result fields.

Hive-mind currently consumes normalized metadata from its embedded adapters, including:

- success / non-zero exit classification
- session id
- usage-limit detection and reset time/timezone
- human-readable result summary
- Anthropic total cost where available
- public pricing estimate where available
- detailed model usage
- stream token usage
- sub-agent call summaries
- execution error classification

The current agent-commander result object has useful `exitCode`, `output.plain`, `output.parsed`, `sessionId`, and `usage` fields, but hive-mind still needs adapter-specific parsing to derive the complete normalized result shape.

Request:

Expose a stable normalized result metadata object from agent-commander for each supported tool (`claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, `agent`) so callers like hive-mind can avoid parsing tool-specific output while still reporting costs, usage limits, summaries, and failures consistently.
